contexts.name: App Shell & Shared Chrome
contexts.description: The authenticated product frame: AppShell composes AppSidebar's two-level rail nav (SectionRailNav) and AppTopbar around each module page, with shell-context syncing the mobile drawer and icon-map resolving module icons. Modal, ModulePage, SectionSkeleton, AppShellSkeleton and NextSteps are the shared dialog/page-header/skeleton/cross-module-link primitives every module reuses; AppSignInGate is the conversion wall shown to anonymous visitors on /app.
contexts.businessFeature: authenticated app shell and navigation chrome

contexts.name: Site Chrome, Auth & Demo Shell
contexts.description: Global site chrome shared across every page: header nav (with journey-aware mobile menu), footer, Cmd/Ctrl+K command palette, locale/theme switchers, doc-style task pager with keyboard hotkeys and visited-journey beacon, Google sign-in/out button, session provider, and a header AI-usage-quota chip. Also includes the public no-auth DemoShell/DemoModule/DemoShellSkeleton, a visual twin of the authed app shell that dispatches to real feature modules for the /dashboard public demo. Shared chrome/shell infrastructure, not a single feature.
contexts.businessFeature: Site navigation, authentication and public demo shell

contexts.name: App shell, dev tooling, design system & site metadata infrastructure
contexts.description: Root Next.js special files that wrap every route (layout, loading, error, global-error, not-found, template), the in-browser DevInspector overlay (dev-only, mounted from the root layout when NODE_ENV=development), the internal design-system swatch/showcase page, and the SEO/metadata generators (manifest.ts, robots.ts, sitemap.ts, opengraph-image.tsx). Cross-cutting app infrastructure rather than product-facing pages.
contexts.businessFeature: Site shell, error boundaries, developer tooling and SEO metadata

contexts.name: Design System Primitives
contexts.description: Shared, dependency-light design-system building blocks used app-wide: Container/Eyebrow/Pill/Button primitives (ui.tsx) documented as the centralisation of ~185 hand-rolled buttons, an inline SVG icon set (icons.tsx), a pure server-renderable Sparkline chart primitive, the framer-motion LazyMotion provider and Kinetics animation primitives (ChartReveal/Tally/Kinetic/Marquee) for marketing surfaces, and a live locale-formatting showcase page component. This is a genuine shared design system, not feature-specific UI; rendered together on /design-system.
contexts.businessFeature: Design system / shared UI primitives

contexts.name: UI Shell: Navigation, i18n & Design Tokens
contexts.description: The cs/en locale system (cookie-backed LocaleProvider + client/server hooks + the typed Messages dictionary + {placeholder} interpolation) paired with the site's navigation/identity model: the single NAV_ITEMS source shared by header/footer/sitemap/quick-nav (nav.ts, with its own slugify/diacritics-normalize helpers), canonical site URL/brand constants (site.ts), the @theme-parsed design-token reader plus its pure WCAG-luminance color math (design-tokens.ts / design-tokens-color.ts), Framer Motion easing presets (motion.ts), and localStorage journey-progress tracking for the case-study walkthrough (journey.ts).
contexts.businessFeature: Site navigation, localization and design system

contexts.name: Core Platform Infrastructure
contexts.description: Cross-cutting server infrastructure the whole app depends on: the locale-aware number/date/currency formatter factory (format.ts) whose default cs-CZ instance ~199 call sites import; the dashboard's PerformanceData/MetricKey type contracts (types.ts) and its static JSON dataset loader (data.ts); the node:sqlite rate-limiter + LOCAL_DB offline persistence (db.ts) gated by the local-mode.ts flag; the Firebase Admin/Firestore singleton (firebase.ts); request-memoized auth session accessors (session.ts) and the ADMIN_EMAILS allowlist (admin.ts); the constant-time Vercel Cron bearer-token guard (cron-auth.ts); and best-effort outbound email/webhook alerting (email.ts) plus CSV/download export helpers (export.ts).
contexts.businessFeature: Shared server infrastructure (data, auth, formatting, ops)

contexts.name: Article & Reporting Publishing Pipeline
contexts.description: The headless-CMS Article content model (typed blocks/FAQ loaded from JSON) and everything that produces or renders it: the shared load-time validator (article-validate.ts) every Article producer runs, the Markdown serializer and the OG share-card renderer, the dashboard-to-AI-prompt snapshot builder (snapshot.ts) and its deterministic, no-AI snapshot-to-Article bridge (snapshot-to-article.ts) that also powers the white-label /m/{slug} microsites (microsite.ts) and the "report to chat" opening summary/chips (report-chat.ts).
contexts.businessFeature: Content publishing and automated performance reporting

contexts.name: Article Reading Experience
contexts.description: Long-form blog/case-study article UI: body renderer with UTM-tagged links, table of contents (desktop + mobile), heading anchors, FAQ permalinks, author bio, breadcrumbs, reading progress bar, print-expand, share bar, markdown copy button and toast, plus small state helpers (permalink, reading-resume, section-store). Feature-specific UI for the /clanek article routes.
contexts.businessFeature: Content marketing / article reading UX

contexts.name: Public marketing & demo pages
contexts.description: Unauthenticated marketing/case-study pages that showcase each product capability with sample data (home, ai-asistent, cena/pricing, and root-level kampane/knihovna/kvalita-modelu/lokalni-seo/mapa/socialni feature pages), the no-login `/dashboard` full-product demo (reuses real module components with mock projects), the public SEO-indexable client microsite (`/m/[slug]`), the public shared-report viewer (`/report/[token]`), and the `/clanek` article/case-study pages including their markdown export route and OG-image generators. All server-rendered; none require a session.
contexts.businessFeature: Public marketing, product demo and client-facing report/microsite viewing

contexts.name: Marketing Landing Pages
contexts.description: Public top-of-funnel marketing pages: the Adamant homepage (BrandLanding, its Crossroad case-study directory and meta data) and the standalone local-SEO showcase page (LocalSeoShowcase with its RankClimbDemo, RankClimbChart, VisibilityGauge and CompetitorBars charts). Feature-specific marketing/landing UI, several pieces using the shared motion Kinetics primitives for scroll-triggered reveals.
contexts.businessFeature: Marketing landing pages

contexts.name: Auth & BYOM entitlements
contexts.description: NextAuth.js v5 config (Google OAuth + Firestore adapter, with a hard-gated DEV_AUTH bypass that only works outside production) and the BYOM (bring-your-own-model) settings API. guard.ts centralizes requireUser/requireByomUser, which keys/matrix/route/validate all import. Every route requires a signed-in user; mutating BYOM key/vendor config additionally requires the BYOM plan entitlement. NOTE: src/app/api/auth/[...nextauth]/route.ts belongs to this context but Vibeman rejects catch-all paths.
contexts.businessFeature: Sign-in and bring-your-own-model key management

contexts.name: Account, Activity Feed, Demo Data, Users & Usage Metering
contexts.description: Account security checklist (account/compute.ts, pure) and session revocation against the NextAuth Firestore `sessions` collection (account/sessions.ts). The project-wide activity feed (activity/compute.ts filtering/rollups, sample.ts illustrative events) is a thin wrapper around the campaigns domain's tenant activity store: emit.ts and live.ts both import recordActivity/listActivity from `@/lib/campaigns/activity` via resolveTenant — i.e. this 'account-level' activity feed has no persistence of its own, it piggybacks on context 1's per-tenant Firestore collection. demo/projects.ts holds in-memory mock Project objects for the public no-auth demo. users/local.ts is the SQLite `users` table (LOCAL_DB mode only, mirrors the Firestore-adapter user). usage.ts meters per-user daily AI-eval/sync quota by plan at Firestore `usage/{userId}`.
contexts.businessFeature: Account security, activity timeline, demo data and usage metering

contexts.name: Account, Settings & AI Model Configuration
contexts.description: The project Nastaveni (settings) surface and account-level admin: ProjectSettings, AccountSecurity (profile, honest security checklist, GDPR deletion request), ActivityModule (project-wide audit timeline), IntegrationStatusModule (per-category connector readiness board), and the BYOM (bring-your-own-model) trio ByomKeys/ByomMatrix plus the read-only ByomQualityMatrix/ByomQualityOverview quality scoreboard, which is also reused on the public /kvalita-modelu page.
contexts.businessFeature: account security, project settings, integration status, and BYOM model quality

contexts.name: AI generation, creative studio & ops telemetry
contexts.description: The core `/api/ai` multi-tool generation endpoint (17 LLM tool modes: ads, chat, brief, monthly recap, twin voice, etc — BYOM-aware, cache-then-quota gated) and its `/api/ai/status` preflight, the Leonardo-backed Creative Studio image endpoints (generate/list/delete, style-prior attribution, background removal, reference upload), the skills marketplace catalog, and the LLM eval telemetry feed. Auth posture is notably mixed: `/api/ai` and `/api/images/upload-ref` are deliberately public/unauthenticated (IP rate-limited only, despite calling paid providers), `/api/skills` is public read-only, `/api/images` requires a session only to persist results, and `/api/eval/telemetry` fails closed to an ADMIN_EMAILS allowlist (platform-wide spend data, not per-tenant).
contexts.businessFeature: AI copy/analysis generation, AI image creative studio, and LLM ops visibility

contexts.name: AI Abuse Guards & Response Governance
contexts.description: Per-IP throttling and spend protection for the unauthenticated, paid AI endpoints: a fixed-window sqlite rate limiter plus a process-level concurrency cap and spoof-resistant client-IP resolution (rate-limit.ts), its Firestore-backed cross-instance twin with a global daily spend ceiling in one transaction (durable-limit.ts) built on framework-free pure decision helpers (durable-limit-core.ts), an in-memory input-hash response cache that dedupes identical /api/ai submits within a TTL window (response-cache.ts), and the pure decision helpers behind the /api/ai/status preflight banner — which provider would serve, demo/exhausted/degraded/low-budget notice kind (status-core.ts).
contexts.businessFeature: AI abuse prevention & budget governance

contexts.name: AI Workspace Contracts, Pipeline & Ad Experiments
contexts.description: The shared client+server contract for all 15 AiMode tools (ai-types.ts: every request/result interface plus the AiResponse/AiMeta/AiError envelopes) and its server-only request validators (validation.ts, one validateXRequest per tool with shared length/enum caps), the pure step-mappers that chain tools into the one-click content pipeline (pipeline.ts: keywords→clusters→brief→article-draft→distribution), the cross-tool handoff mapper (handoff.ts: brief→PPC-ad seed), the per-tool bounded localStorage generation history (history.ts), and the Firestore-backed A/B ad-experiment model plus ROAS/strength-based winner scoring (experiment-types.ts pure model, experiments.ts persistence).
contexts.businessFeature: AI workspace contracts & tool chaining

contexts.name: Diagnostic, Growth & Twin-Voice AI Tools (gate-tracked)
contexts.description: The remaining 9 gate-tracked generateStructured tools: data-grounded diagnostic tools (cohort-diagnosis, lead-source-diagnosis) that read real computed metrics and must not invent figures, SEO/growth generators (keyword-clusters, comparison-outline, lp-variant-ideas, channel-research, onboarding-scan), and the two twin-voice tools (twin-reply drafts an on-brand outbound reply with a confidence/risk autonomy signal, twin-style distils a channel's voice from real message samples plus gap-questions). Each file carries the `// llm-tool: <id>` tag the llm-gate coverage check (findCallSites/checkChokepoint in test-llm/callsites.mjs) and .llm-gate-cache.json enforce.
contexts.businessFeature: Marketing diagnostics & growth generation tools

contexts.name: Core Marketing AI Tools & Skill SDK (gate-tracked)
contexts.description: Ten of the app's 19 gate-tracked generateStructured tools — ads, brief, analysis, campaign-eval, monthly-recap, chat, social, repurpose, local-review-reply, article-draft — plus their shared prompt-fragment helpers (_shared.ts string/list utilities, refine.ts re-run steering notes, voice.ts trained-brand-voice injection shared by twin-reply/social/repurpose) and the tools barrel (tools/index.ts). Also includes the Skill SDK (skills/types.ts: the Skill<I,O> contract + skillToGenerateArgs) and its registry (skills/registry.ts), which wraps ads.ts as the reference plugin and REFUSES to register any skill whose id isn't in GATE_COVERED_SKILL_IDS. Every tool file carries a `// llm-tool: <id>` tag that scripts/llm-gate.mjs cross-checks against test-llm/registry.mjs's LLM_TOOLS array and re-proves incrementally (only the affected tool ids, via --test-name-pattern) through the committed .llm-gate-cache.json whenever that tool's own hashed file changes.
contexts.businessFeature: Marketing content generation tools

contexts.name: BYOM (Bring-Your-Own-Model) Keys & Provider Adapters
contexts.description: Per-user 'bring your own API key' subsystem: AES-256-GCM key encryption/decryption and the store dispatcher (Firestore or local sqlite backend) behind put/delete/resolve-key and the per-operation matrix (keys/*), plus the four vendor HTTP adapters — OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, OpenRouter — that each try native structured output and fall back once to a prompt-embedded schema (byom/adapters.ts), the per-provider reasoning-level mapping (byom/reasoning.ts), the Google-Type→strict-JSON-Schema converter (byom/schema.ts), and the AsyncLocalStorage BYOM request context that generateStructured's resolveProviders() reads to put a configured BYOM key FIRST in the provider order.
contexts.businessFeature: Bring-your-own-model settings

contexts.name: LLM Provider Wrapper, Telemetry & Quality Scoring
contexts.description: The single generateStructured chokepoint (llm/index.ts) that switches between the Claude Code CLI provider (dev) and Gemini API (prod) with bounded retries, one self-repair re-prompt on domain-limit violations, and a deterministic demo fallback, plus its model-tag registry (models.ts), cost estimator (cost.ts), BYOM HTTP-status error classifier (errors.ts), per-request identity AsyncLocalStorage context (request-context.ts), and the eval telemetry/LightTrack mirror/quality-comparison matrix that observe every call. index.ts, claude.ts, gemini.ts and models.ts are exactly the files scripts/llm-gate.mjs hashes in HASHED_FILES to decide whether any tool needs re-proving against the real model.
contexts.businessFeature: AI generation infrastructure

contexts.name: Content, creative & keyword tooling modules
contexts.description: Authenticated `/app/[projectId]` pages for content and creative production: obsah/obsah-plan/obsahovy-engine (content, content plan, content engine), kreativa/produktova-kreativa (creative studio, product creative), knihovna (winning-patterns library), klicova-slova (keyword research), distribuce (distribution), experimenty-lp (landing-page A/B experiments). All render inside the parent layout's session + project-ownership gate.
contexts.businessFeature: In-app content planning, creative generation and keyword research

contexts.name: AI Content & Marketing Tools
contexts.description: Client panels that call the app's AI backend to generate/analyze marketing content: ad copy and experiments, article drafts and content briefs/pipeline, keyword research, creative studio/attribution, performance analysis, and a semantic pattern library derived from campaign results that tunes AI portfolio evaluation. Shares a common primitives.tsx (Field, LoadingTimer, RefineBar, ResultMeta, ToolError) and useAiTool/useAiStatus/usePersistedForm hooks for consistent generate/retry/timeout/history behavior. Feature-specific UI, not a design system.
contexts.businessFeature: AI-assisted marketing content generation

contexts.name: Creative Studio - Image Generation & Revenue Attribution
contexts.description: The generate→recognize→rank image pipeline (images/studio.ts) that calls the Leonardo REST client (leonardo/client.ts: Lucid-Origin txt2img, an SDXL init-image img2img path for product-faithful generation, and background removal) and Gemini vision scoring (leonardo/rate.ts, 1–10 + defects), falling back to deterministic SVG placeholders when LEONARDO_API_KEY is absent; the Firestore-backed creative asset library (images/store.ts: upload bytes to Storage + metadata doc); and the creative-to-ad-revenue attribution model that ranks visual styles by ROAS/vision-score and derives a style prior fed back into the next generation prompt (images/attribution.ts persistence, attribution-types.ts pure scoring, types.ts domain model).
contexts.businessFeature: AI image generation & creative attribution

contexts.name: Twin - Brand Communication Double
contexts.description: The project's per-channel trained-voice + reply-outbox domain module: the TwinState model, autonomy-gate decision rule and client-payload sanitizers (types.ts), the sample↔trained resolution seam that merges a saved voice over the seeded default per scope (resolve.ts) with the untrained per-project-type seed (sample.ts), the six-milestone readiness scorer with prioritized gap hints (readiness.ts), the delivery-connector registry (connectors.ts — currently only a manual no-op sender plus an unimplemented SMTP placeholder), the store dispatcher and its Firestore/local sqlite backends (store.ts, store.firestore.ts, store.local.ts), the tenancy-checked AI-request voice loader consumed by twin-reply/repurpose (load.ts), the stored-voice→wire-shape mapper (wire.ts), and the sessionStorage bridge that carries an inbound message into the reply editor across a route change (reply-seed.ts).
contexts.businessFeature: Trained brand voice & auto-reply outbox

contexts.name: AI Digital Twin (Communication Autopilot)
contexts.description: The three-route Twin feature sharing one persisted blob via useTwinState: TwinModule trains a per-channel voice from real messages and shows the ReadinessRibbon score; TwinChannelsModule sets per-channel autonomy and connector config via TwinChannels; TwinInboxModule is the draft-review-send queue via TwinOutbox, folding in SpeedLeadModule for the dedicated leads inbox. TwinVoiceStudio is the training screen used by TwinModule.
contexts.businessFeature: training and operating an AI communication twin across channels

contexts.name: PPC/Ads Creative Tools, Winning-Pattern Mining & Profitability Targets
contexts.description: PPC/RSA creative tooling plus the business-assumption constants campaign performance is judged against: the client-side "Ad Strength" heuristic and RSA combination sampler/preview, the Google Ads Editor CSV row builders, the single-source channel/category gross-margin tables (margins.ts) and blended-vs-paid PNO/ROAS targets (targets.ts), the SaaS plan/usage-limit catalogue (plans.ts), and the "winning patterns" domain model plus deterministic extractor that mines a tenant's synced campaigns and AI-score history for reusable structure/budget/creative lessons.
contexts.businessFeature: PPC ad creative quality, campaign-derived insights, and profitability assumptions

contexts.name: Campaign Triage, Ad-Ops Control Plane & AI Reporting
contexts.description: Rule-based portfolio triage (triage.ts) driving critical-campaign alerts (alerts.ts) and anomaly alerts (anomaly-alerts.ts) into the durable alert inbox, plus the per-tenant activity timeline (activity.ts). Downstream: deterministic budget-move recommendations (budget-moves.ts, budget-math.ts, simulate.ts) that feed the human-approved, reversible ad-ops control-plane lifecycle (control-plane.ts/-types.ts) which applies moves through the audited mutation path (mutations.ts). Also includes the AI-report support layer: per-tenant white-label report config (report-config.ts/-types.ts), prompt-input formatting for the AI evaluation (report-input.ts), and shareable read-only report links (shared-report.ts, Firestore `sharedReports` collection). NOTE: campaigns/store.ts (context above) locally reimplements `roas = (cost, value) => value/cost` at line 602 instead of importing the shared `roas()` from metrics/ratios.ts (which even has the opposite argument order) — a duplicate ROAS calculator worth consolidating.
contexts.businessFeature: Campaign health monitoring, budget reallocation and client reporting

contexts.name: Campaign Sync & Google Ads Connector
contexts.description: The Google Ads campaign model (types.ts, aggregate/deriveMetrics), the per-tenant Firestore store (campaigns/series/snapshots, sync-over-sync change diff, period-keyed docs), the two-provider connector (sample vs live googleAdsProvider) and the shared sync pipeline used by both the manual and cron sync routes. google/ads.ts is the dependency-free REST client (searchStream, listAccessibleCustomers) these depend on directly; keyword-planner.ts and token.ts are the other Google Ads API surfaces (keyword ideas, OAuth access-token exchange). Persistence is Firestore-only (`tenants/{tenant}` campaigns/series/snapshots subcollections, `adsConnections/{userId}` for connected accounts) — campaigns has no local/SQLite backend variant, unlike most other domains.
contexts.businessFeature: Google Ads campaign performance sync

contexts.name: Campaign performance & ads operations modules
contexts.description: Authenticated `/app/[projectId]` pages for Google Ads campaign management and performance analysis: kampane (campaigns), vykon (performance), zisk (profit), spotreba (spend), ltv, kvalita-leadu (lead quality), rychla-reakce (quick-reaction/control-plane UI), aktivita (activity feed), sprava-kanalu (channel management), kanaly (channels). All render inside the parent layout's session + project-ownership gate.
contexts.businessFeature: In-app campaign performance dashboards and ad operations

contexts.name: Campaign ops & tenant utility/research
contexts.description: The campaign sync/read API and the ad-ops control-plane (create/approve/revert budget change-sets with a guardrail-violation path), plus a cluster of small per-tenant utility and research endpoints: activity feed, alert inbox, usage/plan limits, saved keyword lists, the auto-derived + saved patterns library, and A/B ad experiments. All routes resolve the caller via auth() and degrade gracefully (empty list / sample tenant) for anonymous GETs rather than 401ing, while mutating POSTs require a session.
contexts.businessFeature: Ad campaign sync, budget governance and per-tenant utility feeds

contexts.name: Campaigns / Ad Ops Control Plane
contexts.description: Ad-campaign management feature for the /kampane surface: campaign table with triage/aggregation, budget-move control plane (propose/approve/revert change packages with guardrails), health and score timelines, activity feed, alerts inbox, ads-account picker, microsite cards, shareable/printable reports, and the useCampaigns data hook. Feature-specific UI wired to lib/campaigns/*.
contexts.businessFeature: Ad campaign management and budget control

contexts.name: Finance: LTV, Profit, Spend & Client Reporting
contexts.description: LtvModule (CAC-to-LTV cohort economics, project-type-aware framing) with LtvDiagnosisPanel/LtvProjectionPanel/LtvReportButton; ProfitModule (channel/product margin and budget reallocation); SpendModule (LLM usage cost tracking by operation/model); and MonthlyReport, the client-ready recap that composes CostModelEditor, CompetitorEditor and ReportBeyond (LTV + inventory summary cards) into one AI-narrated monthly report.
contexts.businessFeature: CAC/LTV cohort economics, profit/margin modeling, LLM spend tracking, and the monthly client report

contexts.name: LTV, Spend & Cross-Module Insights
contexts.description: Three smaller pure-compute analytics domains: ltv/compute.ts (CAC/LTV/payback math over acquisition cohorts, geometric retention extrapolation) with its sample cohort generator; spend/{aggregate,compute,live,sample}.ts (spend rollups, no dedicated persistence found in this chunk); and insights/aggregate.ts, the cross-module signal hub that gathers and ranks Recommendations emitted by every project-type module (reads projects/modules.ts and project-data/dataset.ts) for the Overview command center.
contexts.businessFeature: Customer lifetime value and cross-module recommendation aggregation

contexts.name: Cost Model & Profit Analytics
contexts.description: Two independent profit-margin engines: cost-model/compute.ts (`periodProfit`) turns revenue/adCost/conversions into TRUE net profit after COGS + overhead + per-order fulfilment for the monthly report's cost model (persisted per-project via store.ts → store.local/.firestore, SQLite table `cost_model`), while profit/compute.ts applies a per-channel margin model to the channel mix for gross/net profit, POAS and break-even ROAS (overhead.ts, products.ts, trend.ts, types.ts, sample.ts). Both independently compute the same POAS shape (`grossProfit / cost`, gross = revenue × margin) — a duplicate profit-margin calculator worth consolidating into one shared module.
contexts.businessFeature: True net-profit and per-channel margin analytics

contexts.name: Inventory & Warehouse Sync
contexts.description: Stock-pacing math (compute.ts: seasonality index + velocity/on-hand → days-of-cover + run/trim/pause action), the warehouse connector hub (warehouse.ts, providers.ts) fronting three provider integrations — Baselinker (baselinker.ts), generic multichannel/3PL, and ERP systems like POHODA/Money S3/HELIOS (erp.ts) — plus AES-encrypted provider-token storage (token-crypto.ts), the connection store dispatcher (connection-store.ts → .local/.firestore), sync orchestration (sync.ts, sync-health.ts) and sync-failure alerting (sync-alerts.ts) into the action-plan output (action-plan.ts).
contexts.businessFeature: Warehouse/ERP-connected stock pacing and budget actions

contexts.name: Product Catalog: Model, Feed Import & Ad-Copy Generation
contexts.description: The catalog/offering domain: the polymorphic Offering union (product/plan/service, offering.ts), the seed/starter/sample data layers (seeds.ts, starter.ts, sample.ts), the resolve/load seam that picks persisted-vs-seeded data (resolve.ts, load.ts), the local/Firestore store dispatcher pair (store.ts → store.local.ts | store.firestore.ts), and client-input sanitization (validate.ts). Also the feed pipeline: SSRF-guarded feed fetch (feed-fetch.ts), Heureka/Google-Merchant/CSV parsing (feed.ts), import-diff merge (import.ts), a deterministic RSA/PMax asset-group generator (generate.ts) and its CSV/text exporters (export.ts), plus a per-user rate limiter for the write endpoints (rate-limit.ts).
contexts.businessFeature: Product catalog management and Google Ads asset generation

contexts.name: Catalog, Inventory, Audience & Distribution
contexts.description: CatalogManagerModule (the type-aware offering editor other modules read from: products/plans/services with pricing and margin) and CatalogModule (Google Ads asset-group/creative generator); InventorySeasonModule (seasonality + stock-cover pacing) with InventoryBudgetActions (executable inventory-aware budget change-sets) and WarehouseSourceBar (ERP/3PL connector picker); AudienceModule (funnel, segments and revenue); BrandingModule (report/microsite brand accent and logo); and DistributionModule (repurposing one article across channels with UTM tracking and newsletter handoff).
contexts.businessFeature: product/offering catalog management, inventory-aware budget actions, audience funnel, branding, and content distribution

contexts.name: Monthly Report: Live Metrics Ingestion & Tile Model
contexts.description: The account-level (not per-campaign) Ads-data path feeding the monthly report/AI recap: build.ts turns synced daily rows into the PerformanceData shape, map.ts maps Google Ads searchStream rows into MetricRow (reusing google/ads.ts), resolve.ts is the data-source seam picking live-synced-vs-sample data, sync.ts pulls + persists a project's account totals (SQLite table `report_metrics` via store.ts → store.local/.firestore). report/compute.ts defines the per-project-type report tile spec (which metrics show, delta direction) and report/recap-context.ts builds AI-recap grounding text from the cost model and 12-month history — both consume this ingestion layer's output.
contexts.businessFeature: Monthly client report data sourcing and tile presentation

contexts.name: Metrics & Analytics Engine
contexts.description: The pure analytics layer over a project's daily series — the single source of truth every KPI, chart and channel table derives from. ratios.ts holds the shared divide-guarded ratio primitives (roas, pno, aov, cr, cpa, cpc, ctr) explicitly designed so both the dashboard (revenue/visits naming) and the campaigns domain (conversionValue/clicks naming) compute identical numbers — campaigns/types.ts correctly imports from here, but campaigns/store.ts's local `roas()` (see context 1) bypasses it. totals.ts/series.ts/trends.ts/pacing.ts/seasonality.ts/channels.ts/anomalies.ts/meta.ts/snapshot.ts round out the engine; index.ts is the barrel re-export.
contexts.businessFeature: Dashboard KPI and trend computation

contexts.name: Performance Dashboard & Reporting
contexts.description: The 'Výkon' (performance) dashboard: period-scoped KPI grid, trend chart, channel breakdown, goal pacing, weekday profile, anomaly alerts and auto-generated insights (dashboard/vykon/* subpanels), plus a report-to-chat surface that lets a user question a data snapshot via the AI backend. DashboardClient owns shared state and composes the self-contained vykon/ panels. Feature-specific UI for the /dashboard and /app/[id]/vykon routes.
contexts.businessFeature: Marketing performance dashboard

contexts.name: Competitive Intelligence: Keywords, SEO Compare & LP Experiments
contexts.description: Keyword research (opportunity scoring, intent classification, saved keyword lists with negative-keyword aggregation), the "vs./alternative" comparison-query catalog plus acquisition-channel scoring for competitor-comparison SEO pages (seo-compare), the tracked competitor set with its Firestore/local store and AI grounding text (competitors), and landing-page A/B experiment statistics — required sample size, multiple-comparison-corrected significance (lp-exp).
contexts.businessFeature: Keyword research, competitive comparison SEO, and landing-page experimentation

contexts.name: Organic Visibility, Content Distribution & Brand Voice
contexts.description: Zero-ad-spend growth machinery: the organic-channel visibility plan plus tracked checklist (organic-channels), deterministic article-repurposing into channel-native variants with shared UTM stamping (distribution), content-calendar/idea-queue rollups (content-schedule) and topic-cluster coverage/internal-link-debt tracking (content-engine), the pixel-accurate SERP-truncation plus readability/E-E-A-T scorer for AI content briefs (content/seo-score.ts), and the brand-voice/catalog grounding text fed to content & social AI tools (brand) alongside client-report white-label accent-color helpers (branding).
contexts.businessFeature: Organic growth: free-channel visibility, content distribution/scheduling, and brand voice grounding

contexts.name: Local SEO & Map Pack
contexts.description: The local-SEO product surface: map-pack share-of-voice and ranking-ladder math (mappack), the live-vs-sample keyword-rank ladder store/resolver with its Firestore+local dual-store pattern (local-signals), catalog-grounded service-times-locality coverage targets (local/catalog.ts), the review-inbox filter/sort/sentiment engine with saved-reply macros (reviews), and multi-location fleet rollups plus attention-ranking for agencies managing many locations (locations).
contexts.businessFeature: Local SEO, map-pack ranking, reviews and multi-location management

contexts.name: Local SEO, social, reviews, reporting & catalog modules
contexts.description: The remaining authenticated `/app/[projectId]` pages: lokalni/mapa/srovnani-seo (local SEO, map-pack, SEO comparison), socialni/recenze/schranka (social, reviews, message inbox), mesicni-report/report/reporty (monthly recap, client report, reports), twin (AI persona voice), publikum (audience), katalog/sklad-sezonnost (product catalog + inventory seasonality). All render inside the parent layout's session + project-ownership gate.
contexts.businessFeature: In-app local SEO, social/review management, client reporting and catalog/inventory

contexts.name: Local SEO, Map Pack, Leads & Reviews
contexts.description: The location-based growth cluster: LocalModule (service x area coverage gaps and review reputation) with LocalLadderSource/LocalReviews; LocationsModule (multi-location roster with attention-sorted tasks); MapPackModule composing the Leaflet-based MapPackClient with RankLadder's SVG ranking history; LeadQualityModule (cost-per-qualified-lead + source/campaign funnel) with LeadSourceDiagnosisPanel; SpeedLeadModule (inbound-lead SLA and BANT-qualification inbox); and ReviewInbox (a fuller review-reputation triage + AI reply surface reusing the same local-review-reply AI operation as LocalReviews).
contexts.businessFeature: local search dominance, map-pack ranking, lead-quality funnels, and review reputation

contexts.name: SEO, Keyword & Content Workspace
contexts.description: The organic-growth content pipeline: KeywordsModule (research + saved lists, hands selections to Content via a session brief-seed) and ClusterBuilder; CompareSeoModule/CompareSeoTable (comparison-query opportunity scoring with a live client-side weight-tuning panel); ContentEngine (topic clusters + decaying-post alerts) and ContentSchedule (a Google Business Profile post calendar); OrganicChannels (channel research); and LpExperimentsModule/LpVariantIdeasPanel (landing-page A/B results plus AI-suggested challenger variants).
contexts.businessFeature: keyword research, comparison-SEO scoring, content planning, and landing-page experiments

contexts.name: Onboarding, Integrations & Growth Funnel
contexts.description: New-project setup and funnel-health analytics: the type-aware onboarding connector checklist that deep-links into each module and self-completes from real store state (onboarding/steps.ts + progress.ts), the homepage-scan-to-business-profile bridge (site-fetch.ts) with its Firestore/local dual store, the environment- and per-project integration-readiness probes (integrations), newsletter/publisher audience funnel plus revenue-mix and sponsorship rate-card math (audience), and lead-quality/CPL-CPQL funnel scoring with period-over-period drift alerts (lead-quality) plus its AI-recap grounding text (lead-signals).
contexts.businessFeature: Project onboarding, integration readiness, and audience/lead funnel health

contexts.name: Social Command Center & Speed-to-Lead Response
contexts.description: Two customer-engagement modules: the social command center's domain model, connection/publish flow and deterministic per-platform caption/reply drafting (social), and the speed-to-lead inbox's BANT-style qualification scoring, SLA-timed reply drafting, canned-snippet expansion and response-time analytics (speed-lead).
contexts.businessFeature: Social media publishing and rapid inbound-lead response

contexts.name: Social Media Planning
contexts.description: Organic social-media feature at /socialni: SocialClient (account connection, AI-assisted post drafting with brand voice/tone, publishing) and WeekPlanner (a 7-day calendar that batch-generates and schedules AI-drafted posts by orchestrating the existing draft + posts API routes). Feature-specific UI, small and self-contained.
contexts.businessFeature: Social media content planning

contexts.name: Scheduled cron jobs
contexts.description: Six Vercel Cron endpoints (catalog-sync, digest, microsite, report, social, sync) that fan out scheduled work across every connected user/tenant: Ads re-sync with change-aware anomaly alerts, a weekly performance digest (email + webhook + AI-ops rollup), and microsite/report regeneration. All six are guarded by cronAuthorized()/CRON_SECRET checked as a Bearer token — a distinct auth mechanism from the session-based auth() used everywhere else in the API surface.
contexts.businessFeature: Automated portfolio sync, alerting and digesting

contexts.name: Projects, Project State & Project Data Spine
contexts.description: The Project domain model (types.ts: workspace with a `type` driving sidebar modules + KPI preset) and its CRUD store dispatcher (store.ts → store.local/.firestore, SQLite table `projects`); the module registry (modules.ts, 554 lines — declarative per-project-type module/KPI composition) plus guard.ts (auth/type gate for module pages), context.tsx (client active-project provider), icon-keys.ts, name.ts, brief-seed.ts. project-state/ is a small generic per-project/per-key KV store (SQLite `project_state`). project-data/ is the per-project deterministic data spine (dataset.ts derives one PerformanceData from project id/type/name; seed.ts, source.ts the live-vs-sample seam, vary.ts).
contexts.businessFeature: Project/workspace model, module registry and per-project data spine

contexts.name: Project shell, settings & onboarding
contexts.description: The `/app` and `/app/[projectId]` layout/loading/error shells — the former gates on a signed-in session (AppSignInGate otherwise), the latter additionally resolves and owns-checks the project (404 via notFound()) and seeds the client ProjectProvider/AppShell — plus the project home/overview page and its settings surfaces: nastaveni (settings), ucet (account), integrace (integrations), start (onboarding), branding.
contexts.businessFeature: Authenticated app entry, project shell and account/integration settings

contexts.name: Project & tenant workspace: CRUD, data connections, distribution & social publishing
contexts.description: Per-project CRUD (list/create/update/delete) and its many sub-resources — brand-context, catalog + import/sync, competitors, cost-model, local-signals import, metrics sync, onboarding, organic-channels, a generic keyed state store, AI twin voice training, and a warehouse/ERP connection (token encrypted at rest, never returned to the client) — plus tenant-level client-facing distribution (shareable report links, the public microsite toggle) and social account/draft/message/post management. Every route checks ownership via currentUserId()/getProject() or an auth()-derived userId before reading or writing.
contexts.businessFeature: Project settings, connected data sources, client sharing and social publishing

contexts.name: Project Lifecycle, Onboarding & Overview
contexts.description: Creating, switching between, onboarding into, and getting a KPI overview of a project: ProjectsHome (the /app landing/list) and CreateProjectForm's module-package matrix (with create-project-packages/create-project-shared helpers), ProjectSwitcher, the OnboardingModule 'Start' wizard (site scan to catalog to connected data) plus OnboardingProgressCard/DismissOnboarding, and ProjectOverview which renders a single project's KPI band or, with 2+ projects, the cross-project PortfolioCompare table (via overview/compare.ts) with LocationsOverviewSection folded in. SampleDataNote is the shared 'illustrative sample data' trust banner reused across these and other modules.
contexts.businessFeature: project creation, onboarding, and portfolio dashboard

instructions: Context map for systedo-case. Each group is a business domain (domain field); each context is a feature with its files (filePaths) + a technical category. Read this to learn which files belong to which feature BEFORE editing. When you change a context's files, update them here (or run Vibeman's refresh) to keep this fresh.
